demographics.living_situation: lives at home with both parents

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: Our baby is breathing incredibly fast but only taking tiny breaths, his chest is caving in when he breathes, and he can't cough up the mucus that's rattling in his throat. He's had a cold for a couple of days and today he's gone downhill fast and won't feed at all.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He's got much worse over the last 12 hours after a mild cold and we're really frightened by his breathing and the rattling in his throat.

symptom_narrative.onset: started going downhill about 12 hours ago, after 2 days of a mild runny-nose cold
symptom_narrative.duration: rapidly worsening over the last 12 hours
symptom_narrative.character: very fast, shallow breathing with the chest drawing inward; a wet, gurgling, rattling sound in the throat; a weak little cough that doesn't clear anything
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst we've ever seen his breathing — he looks exhausted and floppy but his eyes are still following us
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant and getting worse, no relief
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it just keeps getting worse on its own; he tires more the harder he tries to breathe
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: won't feed or drink at all today, poor suck and swallow, a low temperature earlier, seems worn out
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has a floppiness condition from birth and has been more chesty before, but never like this
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: completely dependent on us; can't lift his head, totally floppy, refusing all feeds

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: he was diagnosed as a baby with spinal muscular atrophy type 1 — a severe floppy-muscle condition; he has always been very floppy and can't move against gravity
past_medical_history.how_managed: specialist neuromuscular and respiratory team, a written SMA care plan, and a home cough-assist machine we were shown how to use

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: no regular breathing or chest medicines

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: young infant fully cared for at home by both parents, who are present and made the call

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: known severe congenital floppy-muscle condition from birth; non-ambulant, fully dependent, but alert and tracking with his eyes (cognitively engaged)

objective_data_offered.value: we counted his breaths and it's around 60-plus a minute, really fast and shallow
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: counted by an anxious parent, not a clinician

objective_data_offered.value: his home oxygen monitor is reading about 91%
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: home fingertip/foot oximeter on a distressed infant — single reading, possible motion artefact

objective_data_offered.value: about 37.6 on the home thermometer earlier
on video his tummy pushes out while his chest wall sucks inward with each breath in; wet gurgly noisy breathing; he looks pale, exhausted and floppy but his eyes track us
his cough is weak and useless and he can't shift the mucus; he's refused every feed today and his suck and swallow seem very weak
his home cough-assist machine is right here but we're scared to use it too hard on him
